frontierComparison.interpretation: The 22-bucket successor q-avoiding source token is consumed with no survivors, but the emitted 24 post-successor boundary expands the open frontier. This is acceptable only as a ranked handoff; singleton q-child descent remains blocked until this assembly selects the whole-boundary next token.

assembledPieces.claim: All 718 source rows and all 111,172,518,226,866,898,571,161,320,153 successor q-avoiding source classes from the 22-bucket successor token have a later square-obstruction child by prime 277.
assembledPieces.boundary: This closes the source token as a no-survivor batch classification, but it emits post-successor root-child and post-successor q-avoiding rank obligations.

assembledPieces.claim: The post-successor square-obstruction children are partitioned into 24 buckets q in {149,151,157,163,167,173,179,181,191,193,197,199,211,223,227,229,233,239,241,251,257,263,269,277}, each with exactly two roots per source class.
assembledPieces.boundary: The 24 buckets are exact post-successor boundaries, not terminal closures.

assembledPieces.claim: The batch cover emits 222,345,036,453,733,797,142,322,640,306 post-successor root children.
assembledPieces.boundary: Those root children remain outside the assembled proof until a later packet compresses, closes, blocks, or ranks them.

assembledPieces.claim: The same 24 post-successor buckets emit 5,058,399,114,142,580,922,880,572,195,875,967 post-successor q-avoiding classes.
assembledPieces.boundary: Those classes require a compression theorem, structural decomposition, impossibility theorem, or deterministic ranked boundary before any singleton q-child descent.

compressionCandidate.name: p443_q97_p479_later_prime_15_bucket_next_prime_17_bucket_post_next_20_bucket_successor_22_bucket_post_successor_24_bucket_rank_compression
compressionCandidate.coveredFamily: All 24 post-successor obstruction-prime buckets emitted by the 22-bucket successor q-avoiding batch cover: 718 source rows, 111,172,518,226,866,898,571,161,320,153 source successor q-avoiding classes, 222,345,036,453,733,797,142,322,640,306 post-successor root children, and 5,058,399,114,142,580,922,880,572,195,875,967 post-successor q-avoiding classes.
compressionCandidate.whyThisCompresses: The q-avoiding cover proves row-uniform first post-successor obstruction and a shared two-root law for every stored source row. The remaining theorem-facing object is therefore the 24-prime post-successor rank surface, not any one q-child.
compressionCandidate.whyNotSingletonDescent: A q149, q151, or later singleton descent would consume only one emitted bucket while the frontier has expanded to 61 obligations. The finite token now available is the whole 24-bucket post-successor rank boundary.
compressionCandidate.failureBoundary: If no common compression theorem or impossibility theorem is visible, emit an exact deterministic 24-bucket ranked boundary packet with all buckets accounted, explicit survivors if any, and the next finite token.

remainingPuzzleBoundary.singletonDescentBlockedUntil: A whole-boundary compression, structural decomposition, impossibility theorem, or exact deterministic 24-bucket ranked boundary consumes the post-successor finite token.

oneNextAction.action: Compress the 24 post-successor obstruction-prime buckets into theorem-facing families, prove a structural rank decrease or impossibility theorem, or emit an exact deterministic 24-bucket ranked boundary before singleton q-child descent.
oneNextAction.failureBoundary: A deterministic ranked boundary packet with all 24 buckets accounted, explicit survivors if any, and the next finite token; no singleton q-child descent is opened by this assembly.
oneNextAction.whyCheaperThanSingleSelector: The 24 buckets share the same row-uniform two-root mechanism and are exact at the rank-token level, so a whole-boundary compression/rank packet is more informative than starting q149/q151/etc. one by one.
oneNextAction.completionRule: The next packet accounts for every one of the 24 post-successor buckets by compression theorem, structural decomposition, impossibility theorem, or exact deterministic ranked boundary.

proofBoundary: This packet is convergence assembly after the 22-bucket successor q-avoiding batch cover. It chooses a whole 24-bucket post-successor compression/ranked-boundary action and blocks singleton q-child descent. It does not close any emitted post-successor bucket, root child, q-avoiding class, unavailable complement, p4217 complement, collision-free matching obligation, or Problem 848.
nextTheoremMove: Compress the 24 post-successor rank buckets, prove a structural/impossibility theorem over the whole post-successor surface, or emit an exact deterministic 24-bucket ranked boundary; do not open q149/q151/singleton descent first.
